reasoning: The polynomial f has degree 4 (expanding gives 2x⁴ - 28x³ + ...). The informal claim states that all (complex) zeros of f are real, with one in each of four specified disjoint intervals — a total of 4 real zeros. For a degree-4 polynomial, "all zeros are real and there are 4 of them in 4 disjoint intervals" is logically equivalent to "f has exactly 4 distinct real zeros." The Lean statement asserts precisely that: there exists a Finset s ⊂ ℝ with |s|=4 that is exactly the set of real roots of f (both containment directions are stated). Although the Lean version doesn't mention the specific intervals, those were used as the IVT argument to establish the count — the ultimate claim "f has 4 distinct real zeros" (equivalently, all zeros are real) is faithfully captured.
